title: Endpoints rely on request.user without requiring authentication
reasoning: Every resource is scoped or created using `request.user`, but these APIView classes do not declare authentication or `IsAuthenticated` permissions locally. If project-level DRF defaults allow anonymous access, anonymous users can hit these endpoints and trigger errors or potentially create inconsistent behavior around ownership checks. The confidence is medium because global permission settings are not included in the reviewed excerpt.
reproduction: With permissive DRF defaults, call any listed endpoint without credentials. GET filters or POST saves operate with `AnonymousUser` rather than rejecting the request with 401/403.
recommendation: Declare `permission_classes = [IsAuthenticated]` on these API views or on a shared base class, unless project-level settings already enforce authentication for all DRF views.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: API/tests.py has no authentication or anonymous-access coverage for these endpoints.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add tests asserting unauthenticated GET/POST requests to each resource list endpoint are rejected with 401 or 403, and authenticated requests remain scoped to the current user.
minimumFixScope: Add explicit authentication permission enforcement for the API views in `API/views.py` or a shared base used by them.
